step_by_step_reasoning: The user starts at the left, sending traffic (300k requests/min) to CloudFront. S3 stores static content served via CloudFront. A Lambda function helps CloudFront with regional routing decisions. From CloudFront, requests go to the Load Balancers (ALB). The ALB routes 20% of traffic to the Legacy Monolith system (running on EC2) and 80% to EKS (running microservices). EKS connects to the data layer, which consists of Mongo/Kafka (running on self-managed EC2) and RDS. CloudWatch is used for monitoring across the architecture.

graph.name: Seera Group (Almosafer) Hybrid Microservices Architecture
graph.notes: Migration from legacy monolith to EKS microservices handling 300k requests/minute. Utilizes a hybrid data layer of managed RDS and self-managed Mongo/Kafka on EC2.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Caches and serves static content globally.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores images, 3D assets, and video content.

nodes.name: Routing Lambda
nodes.notes: Determines viewer region and guides backend routing.

nodes.name: 60+ Load Balancers
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Separated for Web and APIs.

nodes.name: Mongo / Kafka
nodes.notes: Self-managed message streaming and NoSQL database layers.

edges.notes: Writes/reads event streams and NoSQL data.
